channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Theo van Hoytema: Pioneering Dutch Nature Illustrator of the 19th and Early 20th Centuries
item.description: Theo van Hoytema (1863–1917) was a renowned Dutch artist and illustrator celebrated for his exquisite nature illustrations that bridged scientific accuracy with artistic emotion. Born in the Netherlan...

item.title: National Assembly Panel Warns of Poor Internet Quality Despite 5G Rollout in Major Cities
item.description: The National Assembly Standing Committee on Information Technology and Telecommunication expressed serious concerns over the deteriorating state of internet services across Pakistan. Despite 5G spectr...

item.title: Tom Homan Blames Democrats for Delay in ICE Body Cameras After Recent Shootings
item.description: Tom Homan, President Donald Trump’s border czar, faced criticism for his response to recent fatal ICE shootings in Texas and Maine, where agents were not wearing body cameras. Homan claimed the delay ...

item.title: Helicopter Crash Near Vancouver Island Leaves Two Dead, One Injured
item.description: A helicopter crash near north Vancouver Island on Tuesday morning resulted in two fatalities and one injured person, according to West Coast Helicopters. The incident occurred during forestry operatio...

item.title: Trump Officials Seek Public Input to Reform Medicare Payment System
item.description: The Trump administration has initiated a public consultation process to overhaul Medicare's payment system for physicians, aiming to realign financial incentives toward primary care and preventive ser...

item.title: Spain's Penalty Decision Explained After Yamal's Handball Incident
item.description: Spain secured a penalty against France in their 2026 World Cup quarter-final after Lucas Digne tackled Lamine Yamal inside the penalty area. Replays suggested Yamal may have handled the ball before th...

item.title: Key Earnings Reports and Regulatory Updates to Monitor This Week
item.description: The Wall Street Journal's daily stock market update highlights critical events shaping the financial landscape. Key focus areas include corporate earnings reports from major tech firms, Federal Reserv...

item.title: SAPS launches investigation into murder of Gauteng leader Andile Mvuyiselwa Somgaxa
item.description: The South African Police Service (SAPS) has established a multidisciplinary team to investigate the murder of Andile Mvuyiselwa Somgaxa, a prominent Gauteng leader of the March and March Movement. Som...

item.title: Understanding the Swahili Proverb 'Usipoziba ufa, utajenga ukuta'
item.description: The article explains the Swahili proverb 'Usipoziba ufa, utajenga ukuta' which translates to 'If you do not seal a crack, you will build a wall.' It emphasizes the importance of addressing small probl...

item.title: Alex Eala Reflects on Identity and Mental Resilience After Wimbledon Triumph
item.description: In a recent interview, Filipino tennis star Alex Eala emphasized her self-awareness and mental fortitude following her historic Wimbledon run. The article highlights her journey as a young athlete nav...

item.title: ICE Agent Shoots Colombian National During Maine Immigration Operation
item.description: An Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agent fatally shot a Colombian national during an immigration operation in Biddeford, Maine, sparking protests and calls for accountability. The incident o...

item.title: OpenAI's AI Companion Speaker: A Portable, Screenless Device for Smart Home Integration
item.description: OpenAI is developing a screen-free, portable smart speaker designed to function as a personalized AI companion and home computer. The device, expected to launch in 2027, will control smart-home applia...

item.title: British illegal immigrant shot dead by Vietnam veteran during construction work
item.description: A 20-year-old British illegal immigrant, Salis Hanrahan, was shot dead by 75-year-old Vietnam veteran George Barr during construction work at his Philadelphia home. Hanrahan, part of the Irish travele...

item.title: Scientists uncover how seals evolved to hear both in air and water
item.description: Seals possess an extraordinary ability to hear effectively in both air and water, a trait known as amphibious hearing. This adaptation is unique to seals and allows them to thrive in dual environments...

item.title: Clashes Between Protesters and Security Forces During AJK Clearance Operation
item.description: In AJK, clashes erupted between protesters and law enforcers during a clearance operation, resulting in casualties on both sides. The violence occurred in Poonch division, where security forces raided...

item.title: Political Tensions Rise Between CCM and ACT Wazalendo Over Reconciliation Talks
item.description: The article details escalating political tensions between Tanzania's ruling party CCM and the opposition ACT Wazalendo following the release of their joint declaration on July 9, 2026. The conflict ce...

item.title: UK government proposes midnight social media curfew and restrictions for older teenagers
item.description: The UK government has announced plans to introduce an overnight social media curfew for 16 and 17-year-olds, with apps like Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube set to be unavailable by default between midn...

item.title: New Zealand's education reforms face challenge of supporting vulnerable students alongside higher academic standards
item.description: New Zealand is undertaking its most significant education reform in decades, introducing new curricula, revised secondary qualifications and stronger accountability measures for schools. While these c...

item.title: ED probes money laundering linked to illegal betting platforms
item.description: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) has arrested businessman Vikas Garg as part of a probe into alleged money laundering linked to the Mahadev Online Book (MOB) platform and Skyexchange. The investigatio...

item.title: Toronto mayoral candidates address crime concerns after weekend shootings
